name: NTK-aware RoPE Scaling
expansion: Neural Tangent Kernel-aware Rotary Position Embedding Scaling
oneliner: Extends RoPE context by increasing base frequency — preserves high-frequency local resolution while stretching low-frequency global capacity. Works zero-shot.
explanation: Proposed by Reddit user /u/bloc97 (June 2023). Problem: extending context (e.g., 4K→16K) without fine-tuning. PI (Position Interpolation) compresses all frequencies equally, damaging local resolution. NTK insight: different frequency bands encode different scales. High-freq dimensions (local position) should stay untouched; low-freq dimensions (global) should stretch. Increasing base achieves this automatically because $\theta_i = \text{base}^{-2i/d}$ — larger base reduces all frequencies, but proportionally more for already-slow bands. Works surprisingly well zero-shot; better with light fine-tuning.
fundamentals: New base: base' = base · $\alpha$^(d/(d-2)) where $\alpha$ = context extension factor. For $\alpha$=4, d=128: base' $\approx$ 40,440. Effect on wavelengths: highest-freq band (i=0) unchanged ($\lambda_0$=2$\pi$ always), lowest-freq band scales by ~$\alpha$. Intermediate bands interpolate smoothly. Compare PI: all frequencies compressed uniformly by $\alpha$ — damages high-freq resolution. NTK preserves local while extending global.
sources: Formalized in Peng et al., 'YaRN,' arXiv:2309.00071

resources.label: Original Reddit post by /u/bloc97 (NTK-aware scaled RoPE)
